alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework demonstrates flexibility and adaptability to various materials and scales, provided sufficient data are available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study advances future research on different materials and the development of more sophisticated models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study advances future research on different materials and the development of more sophisticated models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The level of complexity addressed in this study is unprecedented in computational materials science, particularly for materials with undefined structures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The level of complexity addressed by this framework is unprecedented in computational materials science, particularly for materials with undefined structures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Minor phrasing difference ('in this study' vs 'by this framework') but substantive claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both human raters and a facial recognition algorithm could predict participants' scores on a political orientation scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both human raters and a facial recognition algorithm could predict participants' scores on a political orientation scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm's predictive accuracy was higher when it leveraged information on participants' age, gender, and ethnicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm's predictive accuracy was higher when it leveraged information on participants' age, gender, and ethnicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same finding about demographic information improving accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model derived from standardized images could predict political orientation from naturalistic images of politicians. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model derived from standardized images could predict political orientation from naturalistic images of politicians. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about transferability to naturalistic politician images is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conservatives tended to have larger lower faces according to the analysis of facial features associated with political orientation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conservatives tended to have larger lower faces according to the analysis of facial features associated with political orientation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The morphological observation is present and identical in both texts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictability of political orientation from standardized images has critical implications for privacy and the regulation of facial recognition technology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictability of political orientation from standardized images has critical implications for privacy and the regulation of facial recognition technology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The stated implications for privacy and regulation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VIDIMU dataset is designed to support affordable patient gross motor tracking solutions for daily life activities recognition and kinematic analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VIDIMU dataset is designed to support affordable patient gross motor tracking solutions for daily life activities recognition and kinematic analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The design intent and contribution are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes 13 activities recorded using a commodity camera and five inertial sensors across 54 subjects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset includes 13 activities registered using a commodity camera and five inertial sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about 13 activities and the sensors but omits the explicit "54 subjects" detail (and uses 'registered' vs 'recorded'), slightly narrowing the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation confirms that a minimally disturbing acquisition protocol can provide a comprehensive picture of human joint angles during daily life activities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation confirms that a minimally disturbing acquisition protocol can provide a comprehensive picture of human joint angles during daily life activities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Validation claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novelty of the dataset lies in the clinical relevance of the chosen movements and the combined utilization of affordable video and custom sensors. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novelty of the dataset lies in the clinical relevance of the chosen movements and the combined utilization of affordable video and custom sensors. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The statement of novelty is consistent across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed F-GNN achieves state-of-art performance with significantly less computation time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FD Graph Neural Network (F-GNN) achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly less computation time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Naming/formatting differs slightly (expanded abbreviation, hyphenation) but the substantive performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: F-GNN offers an excellent trade-off between performance and complexity compared to classical approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: F-GNN offers an excellent trade-off between performance and complexity compared to classical approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about trade-off between performance and complexity is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An appropriately chosen distance-based threshold reduces required training time by roughly 20% with a relatively minor loss in performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An appropriately chosen distance-based threshold reduces required training time by roughly 20% with a relatively minor loss in performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative reduction and trade-off are stated the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Tetra-AML toolbox automates neural architecture search and hyperparameter optimization using the TetraOpt algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TetraOpt algorithm consistently demonstrated superior performance in hyperparameter optimization compared to various optimization algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes TetraOpt's strong HPO performance rather than the preprint's claim about an automation toolbox; the claim is therefore clarified and focused on algorithm performance in HPO.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach shows superior performance compared to Bayesian optimization on the CIFAR-10 dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TetraOpt effectively explores the global optimization space and identifies configurations with higher accuracies.
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a specific empirical comparison (Bayesian on CIFAR-10); the publication reframes this as a general capability to explore the global space and find higher-accuracy configurations, broadening and revising the original comparative statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The compression of ResNet-18 neural networks results in using 14.5 times less memory while losing just 3.2% of accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel iterative method for model compression achieved compression ratios of up to 14.5× for ResNet-18 and 2.5× for ResNet-152.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 14.5× compression for ResNet-18 and adds results for ResNet-152 but omits the specific accuracy-loss detail, thus clarifying and expanding the compression results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework is generic and supports hardware acceleration, including GPUs and TPUs, and can be extended to quantum hardware.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not include statements about general framework support for GPUs/TPUs or extension to quantum hardware, so this preprint claim is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quality-Diversity algorithms are effective in generating repertoires of diverse and efficient policies by relying on the definition of a behavior space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quality-Diversity algorithms are effective in generating repertoires of diverse and efficient policies by relying on the definition of a behavior space.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level contribution statement about Quality-Diversity algorithms without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Policies generated in uncertain environments can lack robustness and repeatability, leading to varied behaviors under slightly different conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this limitation about robustness/repeatability, so the preprint limitation is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MAP-Elites Low-Spread (ME-LS) approach produces consistent and robust policies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ME-LS produces consistent and robust policies.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result directly and with the same emphasis on consistency and robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of ME-LS with the Quality-Diversity Transformer (QDT) yields a single policy capable of achieving diverse behaviors on demand with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of ME-LS with the QDT yields a single policy capable of achieving diverse behaviors on demand with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this combined-method result essentially unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hyperspectral learning is capable of recovering full spectroscopic resolution in the hypercube, comparable to high spectral resolutions of scientific spectrometers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hyperspectral learning is capable of recovering full spectroscopic resolution in the hypercube, comparable to high spectral resolutions of scientific spectrometers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this specific capability (full spectroscopic resolution comparable to spectrometers) directly and with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hyperspectral learning enables ultrafast dynamic imaging, achieving temporal resolutions up to a millisecond using a conventional smartphone camera.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This learning-powered snapshot hyperspectral imaging method yields high spectral and temporal resolutions and eliminates the spatiospectral trade-off.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's device-specific, quantitative temporal claim (millisecond, smartphone) is not preserved; the publication generalizes to high temporal resolution overall and removes the device-specific detail, revising and broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This learning-powered snapshot hyperspectral imaging method eliminates the spatiospectral tradeoff and offers simple hardware requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This learning-powered snapshot hyperspectral imaging method yields high spectral and temporal resolutions and eliminates the spatiospectral trade-off.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert elimination of the spatiospectral trade-off and emphasize high spectral/temporal performance; the publication restates this core methodological advantage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for reliable hypercube recovery and key feature extractions with a transparent learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method allows for reliable hypercube recovery and key feature extractions with a transparent learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion about reliable recovery and transparent algorithms with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most applied practices improved our system, albeit to varying extent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of 10 selected AI engineering practices improved the development of an autonomous stock trading system.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the number of practices and the concrete application domain (autonomous stock trading), narrowing and clarifying the preprint's general statement about improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We were able to overcome all major challenges during the development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All major challenges encountered during the development of the AI-based system were successfully overcome.
alignments.rationale: This claim is carried forward by the publication with essentially the same content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our experiences provide detailed accounts about 10 AI engineering practices, as well as challenges and solutions associated with such a project.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The qualitative results provide detailed accounts of challenges and solutions associated with the application of AI engineering practices.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution as qualitative results that document challenges and solutions; it clarifies the nature of the accounts while preserving the core content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The qualitative results enrich the emerging body of evidence in the field of AI engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiences documented in this study enrich the emerging body of evidence in the field of AI engineering.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution of enriching the evidence base with similar wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study proposes Complementary Pseudo Multimodal Feature (CPMF) for improving point cloud anomaly detection performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Complementary Pseudo Multimodal Feature (CPMF) effectively combines local geometrical information from 3D descriptors with global semantic information from 2D neural networks for point cloud anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from proposing CPMF to asserting its effective combination of modalities for anomaly detection, clarifying the contribution with an effectiveness claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CPMF incorporates local geometrical information in 3D modality and global semantic information in pseudo 2D modality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on MVTec 3D and Real3D datasets demonstrate the complementary capacity between 2D and 3D modality features.
alignments.rationale: The publication supports the architectural claim by reporting experimental evidence of complementarity between 2D and 3D features; the claim is therefore clarified with empirical validation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of CPMF, achieving 95.15% image-level AU-ROC and 92.93% pixel-level PRO on the MVTec3D benchmark.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CPMF achieves a significantly higher object-level AUROC of 95.15% compared to other methods on the MVTec 3D benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a related performance figure (95.15% AUROC) but frames it as object-level and emphasizes comparison to other methods; metric granularity/phrasing differs from the preprint's image-/pixel-level metrics, so the result is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Quasi-Dynamic Parameterisable (QDP) method optimises parameters such as motion velocity and pick and place positions for cloth manipulation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Quasi-Dynamic Parameterisable (QDP) method optimises parameters such as motion velocity and pick and place positions for cloth manipulation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually identical to the preprint claim with no change in content, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By deciding the optimal parameters for manipulation primitives, performance can improve by 20% compared to sub-optimal ones in simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By deciding the optimal parameters for the manipulation primitives, performance can improve by 20% compared to sub-optimal ones in simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative simulation result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-world results demonstrate the advantage of modifying the velocity and height of manipulation primitives for cloths with different mass, stiffness, shape, and size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Real-world results demonstrate the advantage of modifying the velocity and height of manipulation primitives for cloths with different mass, stiffness, shape, and size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim exactly in content and certainty; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed modified algorithm for 2D anisotropic Gaussian filters improves their precision. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed modified algorithm for 2D anisotropic Gaussian filters improves their precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about improved precision with essentially identical wording; only the label moved from conclusion to result, not the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified algorithm is more accurate and robust to noise when applied to synthetic images of fiber bundles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified algorithm is more accurate and robust to noise when applied to synthetic images of fiber bundles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in content and certainty to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the modified algorithm is demonstrated by its application to real-world images of sheet molding compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the modified algorithm is demonstrated by its application to real-world images of sheet molding compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same demonstration claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The physical implementation of artificial neural networks (ANN) fundamentally changes the features of noise influence. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this broad, general claim about physical implementation changing noise features; instead it presents more specific ESN-focused results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals ways to suppress internal noise propagation in recurrent ANN, specifically in echo state networks (ESN). (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noise is less accumulated in echo state neural networks with a diagonal reservoir connection matrix that has a large 'blurring' coefficient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the general statement about suppressing internal noise with a specific structural condition (diagonal reservoir with large blurring coefficient) that explains a way to reduce accumulation, narrowing and clarifying the original claim and with higher stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The noise is less accumulated in ESN with a diagonal reservoir connection matrix that has a large 'blurring' coefficient, particularly for uncorrelated multiplicative noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reduced accumulation of noise in echo state neural networks is especially true for uncorrelated multiplicative noise. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the observation about multiplicative noise but presents it as a more specific/limited result (medium certainty) and separates it from the diagonal/blurrier-matrix formulation, so content is clarified but somewhat narrowed and given lower expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The general view of variance and signal-to-noise ratio of the ESN output signal is similar to that of a single noisy neuron. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general view of variance and the signal-to-noise ratio of the echo state neural network output signal is similar to that of only one neuron. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conceptual comparison but reports it with higher certainty; the content remains essentially the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides an overview of 70 unique design patterns for AI-based systems, including 34 new patterns and 36 traditional ones adapted to this context. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides an overview of 70 unique design patterns for AI-based systems, including 34 new patterns and 36 traditional ones adapted to this context. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative summary claim with identical content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collected design patterns are categorized into popular categories such as 'architecture', 'deployment', 'implementation', and 'security & safety'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the identified patterns have only been mentioned once or twice, indicating that they are not yet widely established. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Although related, these two texts are different claims. The preprint's categorization claim actually matches Q3 in the publication; here the publisher's Q1 is a different (but related) conclusion about frequency of mentions. Mapping below pairs the correct items more precisely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the identified patterns have only been mentioned once or twice, indicating that many patterns are still emerging in the field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Popular pattern categories identified include architecture, deployment, implementation, and security & safety. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: These two claims are related to the pattern corpus but are different in focus; the preprint's frequency/emergence conclusion aligns best with publication Q1, while the categorization claim in the preprint aligns with publication Q3. The mapping below corrects this by pairing each preprint claim to the most similar pub claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrated pattern collection is made accessible through a web-based repository for researchers and practitioners. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The created pattern collection has been integrated into a web-based repository to enhance accessibility for researchers and practitioners. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the same contribution (web-based repository) with equivalent intent and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collected design patterns are categorized into popular categories such as 'architecture', 'deployment', 'implementation', and 'security & safety'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Popular pattern categories identified include architecture, deployment, implementation, and security & safety. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim is effectively the same as the preprint's categorization statement; wording differences are minor and do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the identified patterns have only been mentioned once or twice, indicating that many patterns are still emerging in the field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the identified patterns have only been mentioned once or twice, indicating that they are not yet widely established. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same observation about frequency of mentions and its implication about the patterns' maturity; wording is slightly different but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A diffractive optical network can perform any arbitrary complex-valued linear transformation between its input and output fields-of-view if the total number of optimizable phase-only diffractive features is greater than or equal to ~2 Ni x No. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this specific claim about arbitrary complex-valued (field) transformations; instead it focuses on intensity/time-averaged (incoherent) transformations, so the original field-level claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A spatially-incoherent diffractive optical processor can approximate any arbitrary linear transformation in time-averaged intensity between its input and output fields-of-view. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A spatially incoherent diffractive optical processor can approximate any arbitrary linear transformation in time-averaged intensity between its input and output fields-of-view. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim about incoherent/intensity transformations with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A spatially-incoherent diffractive network can be trained to all-optically perform any arbitrary linear intensity transformation between its input and output if N is greater than or equal to ~2 Ni x No. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A spatially incoherent diffractive network can be trained to all-optically perform any arbitrary linear intensity transformation between its input and output if the total number of optimizable phase-only diffractive features is ≥~2NiNo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim directly mirrors the preprint's condition on the number of diffractive features for intensity transformations; content and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research constitutes the first demonstration of universal linear intensity transformations performed on an input field-of-view under spatially-incoherent illumination. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A diffractive network design can perform all-optical classification of handwritten digits under spatially incoherent illumination, achieving a test accuracy of >95%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a concrete classification demonstration (handwritten digits, >95%) rather than the broader claim of being the first demonstration of universal linear intensity transformations; the claim is therefore made more specific and application-focused.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Spatially incoherent diffractive networks will be broadly useful for designing all-optical visual processors that can work under natural light. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader concluding statement about broad usefulness and operation under natural light does not appear in the preprint claims and thus is an added publication-level conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new adversarial training framework exhibits global consistency, local fine texture details, and flexibility in handling images with free-form holes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new adversarial training framework exhibits global consistency of the repaired image.
alignments.rationale: The preprint bundled three properties into one claim; the publication splits them and the matched pub claim covers only the global-consistency aspect, so the original claim is presented in a more narrowly stated form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed textural and semantic contrastive learning losses stabilize and improve the inpainting model's training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed contrastive learning losses improve the inpainting model's training by better guiding repaired images towards real image data points.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that contrastive losses improve training; the publication frames the improvement in terms of guidance toward real data points, which clarifies the mechanism but does not contradict the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contrastive losses guide the repaired images to move closer to ground truth images and farther from corrupted images in the feature representation space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed contrastive learning losses improve the inpainting model's training by better guiding repaired images towards real image data points.
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives a specific feature-space interpretation (closer to ground truth / farther from corrupted); the publication states a more general guidance toward real image data, so the specificity is reduced but the core claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the model's effectiveness and superiority both qualitatively and quantitatively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed contrastive learning losses improve the inpainting model's training by better guiding repaired images towards real image data points.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports improved training/results attributable to the contrastive losses, which aligns with the preprint's claim of experimental effectiveness, though the publication splits and frames results across several focused claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can be harnessed to increase sustainability in production and product usage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can be harnessed to increase sustainability in production and product usage.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to recognize the wear state of machining tools and rotating X-ray anodes using deep-learning-based computer vision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to recognize the wear state of machining tools and rotating X-ray anodes using deep-learning-based computer vision.
alignments.rationale: The methods and result claim are carried over directly into the publication with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The emissions of CO2 equivalents are reduced by 12% for machining tools and by 44% for rotating anodes as a result of the proposed approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The emissions of CO2 equivalents are reduced by 12% for machining tools and by 44% for rotating anodes as a result of the proposed approach.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative emission-reduction results are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting mean dice coefficients of 0.631 and 0.603 demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate these specific Dice coefficient numbers or the feasibility claim tied to them; the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work can serve as a guideline and inspire researchers and practitioners to utilize computer vision in similar scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting insights from wear state detection serve as a basis for novel product-service systems with improved integration and result orientation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution from a general inspirational/guideline role to a more concrete basis for novel product-service systems, changing the emphasis and scope of impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system is capable of extracting and ranking hypernyms for a given financial term.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system is capable of extracting and ranking hypernyms for a given financial term.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system has been trained with financial text corpora obtained from various sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system has been trained with financial text corpora obtained from various sources.
alignments.rationale: Training-data provenance is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel approach used to augment the training set with negative samples leverages the hierarchy present in FIBO.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention this specific negative-sampling augmentation leveraging FIBO hierarchy, so the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system performs better than existing systems and is also scalable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system performs better than existing systems and is also scalable.
alignments.rationale: Performance and scalability claims are preserved in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed decentralized MARL algorithm demonstrates near-optimality of its policy updates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed decentralized MARL algorithm achieves near-optimality in its policy updates.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same algorithmic near-optimality claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a closed-form characterization of convergence probabilities over the joint policy space for a general class of best-reply based algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a closed-form characterization of convergence probabilities over the joint policy space for a general class of best-reply based algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The technical convergence characterization is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research focuses on multi-agent learning in stochastic games with general state spaces and an information structure where agents do not observe each other's actions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research focuses on multi-agent learning in stochastic games with general state spaces and an information structure where agents do not observe each other's actions.
alignments.rationale: The scope and focus of the research are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Promptable Game Model (PGM) allows users to play games by prompting them with high- and low-level action sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Promptable Game Model (PGM) allows users to play games by prompting them with high- and low-level action sequences.
alignments.rationale: The core functionality of PGM is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our PGM unlocks a director's mode where users can specify goals for agents using prompts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PGM enables a director's mode where users can specify goals for agents using prompts.
alignments.rationale: The director's mode capability is preserved with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method significantly outperforms existing neural video game simulators in terms of rendering quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method significantly outperforms existing neural video game simulators in terms of rendering quality.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim about rendering quality is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present newly collected, annotated, and calibrated Tennis and Minecraft datasets to foster future research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present newly collected, annotated, and calibrated Tennis and Minecraft datasets to foster future research.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GiveMeLabeledIssues is a tool that mines project repositories and labels issues based on the skills required to solve them. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed asynchronous multistage deep reinforcement learning (AMS-DRL) approach enables drones to avoid attacks and reach their targets by adapting to the behavior of multiple evolved pursuers. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution/result; only label (contribution vs conclusion) differs but content and certainty are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMS-DRL guarantees convergence by ensuring Nash equilibrium among agents, as demonstrated through game-theory analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMS-DRL guarantees convergence by ensuring Nash equilibrium among agents, as demonstrated through game-theory analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The game-theoretic convergence claim is echoed exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method outperforms baselines with higher navigation success rates in extensive simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AMS-DRL method outperforms baseline methods with higher navigation success rates in extensive simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Physical experiments validate the effectiveness of the trained policies in real-time flights. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Physical experiments validate the effectiveness of the trained policies in real-time flights. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The real-world flight validation is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of parameters such as relative maximum speed shows an impact on navigation performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of parameters such as relative maximum speed shows an impact on navigation performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural spelling correction model built on Google OCR-ed Tibetan Manuscripts auto-corrects OCR-ed noisy output. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Transformer + Confidence score mechanism architecture is superior to Transformer, LSTM-2-LSTM, and GRU-2-GRU architectures based on Loss and Character Error Rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Transformer + Confidence Score mechanism architecture proves superior to the Transformer, LSTM-to-LSTM, and GRU-to-GRU architectures according to the Loss and Character Error Rate metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the superiority claim for the Transformer+Confidence architecture with the same metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's robustness was examined by analyzing erroneous tokens and visualizing Attention and Self-Attention heatmaps. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate this specific robustness analysis claim; instead it adds a different, unrelated statement about digitization efforts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework combines the optimization-driven and model-free advantages of deep reinforcement learning with stability guarantees from the Youla-Kucera parameterization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework combines the optimization-driven and model-free advantages of deep reinforcement learning with stability guarantees from the Youla-Kučera parameterization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a neural network to express a parameterized set of nonlinear stable operators enables seamless integration with standard deep learning libraries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a neural network to express a parameterized set of nonlinear stable operators allows for seamless integration with standard deep learning libraries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach was demonstrated on a realistic simulation of a two-tank system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach was demonstrated on a realistic simulation of a two-tank system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The simulation demonstration appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed learning rate adaptation mechanism allows the CMA-ES with default population size to effectively solve multimodal and/or noisy problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed learning rate adaptation mechanism allows the CMA-ES with default population size to effectively solve multimodal and/or noisy problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about learning rate adaptation enabling effective solving of difficult problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CMA-ES can be used without hyperparameter tuning, but hyperparameter settings still significantly impact performance on difficult tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the proposed learning rate adaptation, the CMA-ES does not require expensive learning rate tuning to perform well on difficult tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's general statement into a clarified claim that, with the proposed adaptation, expensive tuning is not required; certainty is increased and the scope is narrowed to the adaptation context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate that the CMA-ES with the proposed learning rate adaptation performs better than the CMA-ES with a fixed learning rate on challenging optimization problems. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hyperparameter settings of the CMA-ES significantly impact its performance on challenging optimization tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the importance of hyperparameter settings (matching part of the preprint's result); the publication frames this as a broader contribution and reports higher certainty for the adaptation's practical benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel framework for simulating spin models demonstrates practicality and scalability in modeling the Ising model, the Potts model, and the Cellular Potts model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel framework for simulating spin models using differentiable programming demonstrates practicality and scalability in modeling complex systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim of practicality and scalability but adds the differentiable-programming framing and broadens the target from specific spin models to 'complex systems', so the claim is clarified and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework allows for the optimization of spin models by adjusting the parameters of a system according to a defined objective function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework allows for the optimization of spin models by adjusting the parameters of a system according to a defined objective function.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this optimization capability verbatim, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adapting the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm to a differentiable programming paradigm significantly enhances computational speed through parallel processing capabilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapting the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm to a differentiable programming paradigm significantly enhances computational speed through parallel processing capabilities.
alignments.rationale: The result about the Metropolis-Hastings adaptation and speedup is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can be implemented on different hardware architectures, including GPUs and TPUs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can be implemented on different hardware architectures, including GPUs and TPUs.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the framework's implementability on GPUs and TPUs with comparable certainty, so this contribution is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Denoise Transformer achieves competitive performance on the real-world SIDD benchmark with PSNR/SSIM scores of 50.62/0.990.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Denoise Transformer achieves competitive performance on the real-world SIDD benchmark with PSNR/SSIM scores of 50.62/0.990.
alignments.rationale: The benchmark numbers and claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Denoise Transformer outperforms existing self-supervised methods that rely on convolutional neural networks by addressing issues such as color shifts and texture loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Denoise Transformer outperforms existing self-supervised methods that rely on convolutional neural networks by addressing issues such as color shifts and texture loss.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative claim about outperforming CNN-based self-supervised methods with the same motivations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Context-aware Denoise Transformer (CADT) effectively combines global and local feature extraction to improve image denoising.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Context-aware Denoise Transformer (CADT) effectively combines global and local feature extraction to improve image denoising.
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing CADT's combination of global and local features is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Secondary Noise Extractor (SNE) is designed for low computation while facilitating secondary global noise extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Secondary Noise Extractor (SNE) is designed for low computation while enabling secondary global noise extraction.
alignments.rationale: Only a minor wording change (facilitating -> enabling) appears; the intent and scope of SNE remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Denoise Transformer performs well on blurred textures and low-light images without requiring additional knowledge about noise characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Denoise Transformer does not require additional knowledge about noise level or type to perform effectively on various datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim more generally (various datasets, noise level/type) rather than specific blurred/low-light cases, broadening and clarifying the original claim about not needing noise priors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) increases the interpretability of models and makes them more trustworthy and transparent.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reiterate this general positive claim about XAI benefits; it focuses on limitations and the proposed proxy instead, so the specific claim is effectively omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many XAI methods, including SHAP and LIME, assume that predictors in machine learning models are independent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XAI methods often do not consider multicollinearity or assume feature independence, which can undermine the robustness of their outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation about feature independence/multicollinearity, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assumption of independence among predictors undermines the robustness of XAI outcomes, such as the list of informative predictors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XAI methods often do not consider multicollinearity or assume feature independence, which can undermine the robustness of their outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly links the independence assumption to undermining robustness, directly matching this preprint limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach modifies the outcome of any XAI feature ranking method to account for the dependency among predictors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed proxy modifies the outcome of any XAI feature ranking method to account for the dependency among features.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this proposal with higher certainty (phrased as a proxy and with high certainty), so the claim is preserved but with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is model-agnostic and simplifies the calculation of each predictor's impact in the presence of collinearity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed proxy modifies the outcome of any XAI feature ranking method to account for the dependency among features.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the proxy as applicable to any feature ranking method (implying model-agnostic behavior) and states it with higher certainty; thus the idea is clarified and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant advancements in Semantic Shift Detection have been made using word contextualised embedding models over the past three years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational approaches based on Natural Language Processing have gained increasing attention to automate Lexical Semantic Change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The article reviews measures for change assessment and compares approaches on performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural scene representation learns albedos, normals, and volume densities of the underwater environment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model successfully predicts 19F and 13C NMR chemical shifts of small molecules in specific solvents. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MARBLE decomposes on-manifold dynamics into local flow fields and maps them into a common latent space using unsupervised geometric deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We discover emergent low-dimensional latent representations that parametrize high-dimensional neural dynamics during gain modulation, decision-making and changes in the internal state. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive benchmarking demonstrates state-of-the-art within- and across-animal decoding accuracy of MARBLE compared to current representation learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results suggest that a manifold structure provides a powerful inductive bias to develop decoding algorithms and assimilate data across experiments. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A systematic and quantitative evaluation of methodologies for human movement analysis is required to define the current state of the art. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Seven datasets recorded using inertial-based motion capture were created to support research in human motion modeling, analysis, and generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models generated allow for accurate representation of human professional poses and describe how body joints cooperate and change over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gesture Operational Model accurately generates human professional poses and describes how body joints cooperate and change over time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biases in large-scale language models like ChatGPT originate from various factors including training data, model specifications, algorithmic constraints, product design, and policy decisions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biases in large-scale language models originate from factors such as training data, model specifications, algorithmic constraints, product design, and policy decisions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The causal attribution of biases to the listed factors is preserved verbatim aside from minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article explores ethical concerns arising from the unintended consequences of biased model outputs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deployment of biased language models across diverse applications can lead to unintended ethical implications. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's exploration as a concrete result about deployment leading to ethical implications—the substantive point about unintended ethical concerns is preserved but stated more concretely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are potential opportunities to mitigate biases in generative language models, but some biases are inevitable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some biases in language models are inevitable and persist despite mitigation efforts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article emphasizes the need for a multi-disciplinary, collaborative effort to develop more equitable, transparent, and responsible AI systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current approaches for identifying, quantifying, and mitigating biases in language models require a collaborative, multidisciplinary effort. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Progressive Shift Ladder Transformer (PSLT) requires significantly less computing resources, with nearly 1/3 the amount of parameters and FLOPs compared to existing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Progressive Shift Ladder Transformer (PSLT) requires less computing resources, including a relatively small number of parameters and FLOPs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that PSLT uses fewer resources but removes the specific 'nearly 1/3' quantitative comparison, making the statement less specific (tempered) but keeping the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ladder self-attention block effectively models long-range interactions while maintaining a relatively small number of parameters and FLOPs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ladder self-attention block reduces computational cost by modeling local self-attention in each branch.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from modeling long-range interactions to modeling local self-attention per branch and cost reduction, altering the mechanism described (revised) and narrowing focus to local attention.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PSLT achieves a top-1 accuracy of 79.9% on the ImageNet-1k dataset, which is comparable to models with over 20M parameters and 4G FLOPs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PSLT achieves a top-1 accuracy of 79.9% on the ImageNet-1k dataset with 9.2 M parameters and 1.9 G FLOPs.
alignments.rationale: The accuracy number is unchanged but the publication updates the resource numbers (9.2M, 1.9G) and removes the earlier comparison to much larger models, changing the quantitative framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The progressive shift mechanism in the ladder self-attention block enlarges the receptive field by modeling diverse local self-attention for each branch.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ladder self-attention block reduces computational cost by modeling local self-attention in each branch.
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights local self-attention per branch but does not explicitly claim receptive-field enlargement via a progressive shift; thus the mechanism description is presented more narrowly/without the receptive-field claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture-preserving V-polytope provable repair guarantees that the repaired DNN satisfies the given specification on the infinite set of points in the given V-polytope.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture-preserving V-polytope provable repair guarantees that the repaired DNN satisfies the given specification on the infinite set of points in the given V-polytope.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same guarantee verbatim; there is no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The repair method runs in polynomial time and supports DNNs with various activation functions and architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The repair method runs in polynomial time and supports DNNs with various types of layers, including fully-connected, convolutional, pooling, and residual layers.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the support claim by listing layer types (making the compatibility claim more specific) rather than the more generic 'activation functions and architectures.'

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first provable repair approach that combines architecture preservation with the features of V-polytopes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first provable repair approach that combines architecture preservation with the features of V-polytope repair.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same novelty claim about combining architecture preservation with V-polytope features; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implemented tool APRNN demonstrates better efficiency, scalability, and generalization compared to prior provable repair methods like PRDNN and REASSURE.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implemented tool APRNN demonstrates better efficiency, scalability, and generalization compared to prior provable repair methods PRDNN and REASSURE.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same empirical claim about APRNN's comparative performance against prior methods; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed visualization design for scrollytelling effectively explains AI concepts to non-technical users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visualization design for scrollytelling effectively explains AI concepts to non-technical users.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim exactly; no change in content or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scrollytelling created to explain the Siamese Neural Network demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scrollytelling created to explain the Siamese Neural Network demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed design.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The visualization based on the novel design improves non-technical users' perception and knowledge acquisition of machine learning concepts compared to traditional materials.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visualization based on the novel design improves non-technical users' perception and knowledge acquisition of machine learning concepts compared to traditional materials.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the experimental/result claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spintronic physical reservoir demonstrates autonomous long-term prediction capabilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Magnetic skyrmion confined to a fixed geometry can work as a reservoir computing system and perform autonomous long-term prediction of temporal data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates that the skyrmion-based reservoir can perform autonomous long-term prediction; this preserves the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed reservoir can model chaotic time series and dynamic time-series data effectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Magnetic skyrmion confined to a fixed geometry can work as a reservoir computing system and perform autonomous long-term prediction of temporal data.

alignments.rationale: The preprint suggests broad potential as a general future framework, while the publication frames U-Next as a specific design addressing a gap; this narrows the claim and the publication expresses stronger certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a multi-level deep supervision mechanism in U-Next facilitates network optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific mechanism (multi-level deep supervision) not asserted as a distinct claim in the provided preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework demonstrates the learning of underlying device physics by mapping device structure images to their corresponding Current-Voltage characteristics using variational autoencoders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrates the learning of underlying device physics by mapping device structure images to their corresponding Current-Voltage characteristics using variational autoencoders.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim with matching wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can be quickly deployed on any new device and measurement without requiring domain expertise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can be quickly deployed on any new device and measurement without requiring domain expertise.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework successfully performs inverse design and forward prediction for device structures and their electrical characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework successfully performs inverse design and forward prediction for device structures and their electrical characteristics.
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and phrasing between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine is robust against noise in the inputs and is not confused by weak and irrelevant independent variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine is robust against noise in the inputs and is not confused by weak and irrelevant independent variables.
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is carried over identically from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed online meta-learning framework allows the Edge Device to predict the correctness of the S-ML inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed online meta-learning framework allows the Edge Device to predict the correctness of the S-ML inference.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of enabling edge devices to predict S-ML correctness is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The maximum softmax value output by S-ML is used to decide whether to offload a data sample for L-ML inference.
alignments.rationale: The specific decision rule (max softmax threshold for offloading) is not reflected among the publication claims provided, indicating it was omitted from the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms demonstrate sublinear regret bounds without any assumption on the smoothness of the loss function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HIL-F algorithm achieves a sublinear regret bound of $\text{sqrt}{n\text{ln}(1/\lambda_{\text{min}})/2}$ in a full feedback scenario.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific sublinear regret bounds for particular algorithms (HIL-F), elaborating the preprint's general claim of sublinear regret; this is a more specific/clarified formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed algorithms is evaluated and benchmarked for image classification using four datasets.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation-on-four-datasets claim does not appear among the publication claims provided, so it is not reflected in the publication claim list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The HIL-N algorithm has a regret bound of $O(n^{2/3}\text{ln}^{1/3}(1/\lambda_{\text{min}}))$ in a no-local feedback scenario.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific regret bound for a second algorithm (HIL-N) that was not explicitly stated in the provided preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of Hierarchical Inference (HI) is infeasible due to the unknown correctness of S-ML inference to the Edge Device.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a limitation (infeasibility of HI) that was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an imminent need for guidelines and standard test sets to allow direct and fair comparisons of speech emotion recognition (SER).
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an imminent need for guidelines and standard test sets to allow direct and fair comparisons of speech emotion recognition (SER).
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the need for guidelines and standard test sets is carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Published work reveals a wide range of assumptions and variety in the use of the IEMOCAP database that challenge reproducibility and generalization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Published work reveals a wide range of assumptions and variety in the use of the IEMOCAP database that challenge reproducibility and generalization.
alignments.rationale: The result about variability in prior work on IEMOCAP is reiterated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work provides a set of SER evaluation guidelines based on an analysis of the recent literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work provides a set of SER evaluation guidelines based on an analysis of the recent literature.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of providing evaluation guidelines is preserved verbatim in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We focus on reproducibility assessment in SER using recent publications with open-sourced implementations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We focus on reproducibility assessment in SER using recent publications with open-sourced implementations.
alignments.rationale: The contribution and focus on reproducibility with open-sourced implementations is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a forest dataset called FinnWoodlands, which includes RGB stereo images, point clouds, and sparse depth maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FinnWoodlands dataset consists of RGB stereo images, point clouds, and sparse depth maps, along with ground truth manual annotations for semantic, instance, and panoptic segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dataset modalities and adds explicit mention of ground-truth annotations, which clarifies and expands the original dataset description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FinnWoodlands comprises a total of 4226 objects manually annotated, with 2562 objects (60.6%) corresponding to tree trunks classified into three different instance categories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FinnWoodlands includes a total of 4226 objects manually annotated, with 2562 objects (60.6%) corresponding to tree trunks classified into three different instance categories.
alignments.rationale: The numerical composition and annotation statistics are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset can be used in forestry applications where a holistic representation of the environment is relevant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset can be utilized in forestry applications where a holistic representation of the environment is relevant.
alignments.rationale: The potential application in forestry with holistic environment representation remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An initial benchmark using three models for instance segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and depth completion is provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An initial benchmark using three models for instance segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and depth completion is provided to illustrate the challenges of unstructured scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The benchmark claim is preserved, but the publication frames the benchmark more as an illustration of challenges and marks the claim with lower certainty (medium), so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper illustrates the challenges that unstructured scenarios introduce in forestry applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An initial benchmark using three models for instance segmentation, panoptic segmentation, and depth completion is provided to illustrate the challenges of unstructured scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly ties the benchmark to illustrating challenges in unstructured scenarios, matching this preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KG practitioners experience critical challenges when creating, exploring, and analyzing KGs that could be alleviated through visualization design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KG practitioners experience critical challenges when creating, exploring, and analyzing KGs that could be alleviated through visualization design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claims are textually identical and convey the same result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are three major personas among KG practitioners - KG Builders, Analysts, and Consumers - each with distinct expertise and needs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are three major personas among KG practitioners: KG Builders, Analysts, and Consumers, each with distinct expertise and needs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The persona categorization and wording are effectively the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KG Builders would benefit from schema enforcers, while KG Analysts need customizable query builders that provide interim query results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KG Builders would benefit from schema enforcers, while KG Analysts need customizable query builders that provide interim query results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for Builders and Analysts is identical in content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KG Consumers face a lack of efficacy for node-link diagrams and require tailored domain-specific visualizations to promote KG adoption and comprehension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KG Consumers face a lack of efficacy for node-link diagrams and require tailored domain-specific visualizations to promote KG adoption and comprehension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same observation about Consumers and visualization needs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implementing KGs effectively in practice requires both technical and social solutions that are not addressed with current tools, technologies, and collaborative workflows. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implementing KGs effectively in practice requires both technical and social solutions that are not addressed with current tools, technologies, and collaborative workflows. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about needing technical and social solutions is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed geometrically inspired kernel-based approach mitigates the accuracy-loss issue in classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed geometrically inspired kernel-based approach mitigates the accuracy-loss issue in classification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution/claim is the same; only the label (contribution vs conclusion) differs, not the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel distance measure developed in this approach hides privacy-sensitive information and improves the privacy-utility tradeoff. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel distance measure improves the privacy-utility tradeoff by significantly reducing the risk of membership inference attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the mechanism and attack class (membership inference) behind the privacy gains, clarifying rather than contradicting the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated through experiments on multiple datasets, including MNIST and a real biomedical dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated through experiments on multiple datasets including MNIST and a real biomedical dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation on the same datasets is reported in both versions with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach remains computationally practical. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach remains computationally practical. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the approach is computationally practical without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy-loss due to data being distributed in federated learning is either marginal or not significantly high. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy-loss due to data being distributed in federated learning is either marginal or not significantly high. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The assessment of accuracy-loss in federated settings is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using ChatGPT without human intervention may be inadequate due to reliability related issues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using ChatGPT without human intervention may be inadequate due to reliability issues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same result about reliability concerns with fully automated use of ChatGPT.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With careful design, ChatGPT may outperform human experts in STPA results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With careful design, ChatGPT has the potential to outperform human experts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's STPA-specific claim to a broader potential to outperform human experts, widening the scope of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No statistically significant differences are found when varying the input semantic complexity or using common prompt guidelines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No statistically significant differences were observed when varying the input semantic complexity or using domain-agnostic prompt guidelines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding but uses 'domain-agnostic prompt guidelines' instead of 'common prompt guidelines', a minor clarification of wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a necessity for developing domain-specific prompt engineering. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STPA-specific prompt engineering produced statistically significant and more pertinent results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint recommends domain-specific prompt engineering broadly; the publication provides concrete evidence for STPA-specific prompts, narrowing the scope but strengthening confidence with a statistical result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future challenges include concerns about LLM trustworthiness and the necessity for standardisation and regulation in this domain. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This explicit discussion of future challenges, standardisation, and regulation does not appear in the publication claims list and so is treated as removed from the claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT generally yielded more conservative and less comprehensive outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about ChatGPT producing more conservative and less comprehensive outputs that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prototype device developed for dynamic gaze and accommodation measurements is suitable for use in AR and ophthalmology applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The eye model developed in ZEMAX matches the experiments well. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The eye-model developed in Zemax matches the experiments well. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The modeling result is preserved with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts accommodation from 4 diopters to 1 diopter with better than 0.25D accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicts accommodation from 25cm to infinity (4 diopters) with better than 0.25D accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies and extends the prediction range (25cm to infinity / 4D to 0D) compared with the preprint's narrower 4D to 1D range.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repeatability tests showed accurate gaze and accommodation estimations from subjects. (certainty=high, type=result)
